message:
Sync beads issue database and add proptest regression seeds

- Refresh JSONL issue state with latest status updates
- Check in proptest-regressions/lib.txt for the franken_decision
  module so that previously-discovered failure cases (calibration
  edge case at 0.0 and loss matrix with sparse values) are
  automatically re-tested on every run

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:

 {"id":"bd-31kgp","title":"Fix clippy warnings blocking CI","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"FuchsiaCliff","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:09:20.610194344Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T19:14:04.976929123Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T19:14:04.976804160Z","close_reason":"Fixed: assert!(false,...) -> panic!() in region.rs:1514 and clone_on_copy -> deref in runtime_e2e.rs:1923. The e2e_messaging.rs issue was fixed by CrimsonBridge. All clippy checks pass.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-31nlw","title":"[bd-2ijqf] RuntimeState API Migration + Callsite Audit","description":"Goal: migrate all RuntimeState callsites to the new Task/Region/Obligation table APIs and eliminate lingering full-state locks in hot paths.

Background:
- After TaskTable + Region/Obligation tables exist, any leftover RuntimeState locks will reintroduce contention and defeat the refactor.
- Multiple modules outside scheduler (e.g., io_op, runtime builder, lab runtime) access RuntimeState; these must be audited and updated.
- Cross-table refactors (bd-2utc3) must land before this audit, otherwise we will audit against stale paths.

Scope / Deliverables:
- Inventory all callsites of RuntimeState locks and categorize by hot-path vs cold-path.
- Update callsites to use TaskTable/RegionTable/ObligationTable APIs as appropriate.
- Remove or fence any remaining full-state lock usage in hot paths (no compatibility shims).
- Ensure tests and benches use the correct APIs; update helper utilities and test harnesses.

Testing & Logging:
- Add unit tests for any new helper APIs introduced during migration.
- Use lock-metrics in tests to assert hot paths do not acquire RuntimeState locks.
- Run E2E contention harness once after migration to confirm logs still emit correctly.

Acceptance Criteria:
- No hot-path code holds a full RuntimeState lock.
- All callsites compile cleanly with new table APIs; no wrapper shims.
- Migration steps documented in the bead notes for future maintenance.","acceptance_criteria":"1. No hot-path code holds a full RuntimeState lock (verified by audit list + structured test logs).
2. All callsites compile cleanly with new table APIs; no shims or compatibility wrappers.
3. New helper APIs have unit tests; migration changes are covered by lab tests where relevant.
4. E2E contention harness (bd-ecaeo) passes; add/extend an E2E script if new hot-path APIs are introduced.
5. Migration steps documented in bead notes for future maintenance.","notes":"[2026-02-06] Inventory (DarkElk): RuntimeState big-lock callsites to migrate once bd-2utc3/bd-s13um land. Hot-path (worker loop): src/runtime/scheduler/worker.rs (execute: state.lock at ~74, 201, 230, 283, 375, 386, 408, 418). Hot-path (queue ops): src/runtime/scheduler/local_queue.rs (push/pop/steal: state.lock at ~91, 99, 146, 185; plus test helpers ~272/480). Hot-path (scheduler inject/wake/spawn): src/runtime/scheduler/three_lane.rs (many state.lock in inject_cancel/timed/ready/spawn/wake, plus worker init). Spawn API path (potentially hot depending on workload): src/runtime/builder.rs (RuntimeInner::spawn: state.lock at ~1069). Cold/setup: src/runtime/builder.rs (root region + config at ~962; blocking pool handle at ~1039).

Non-scheduler RuntimeState usages that will need audit/adjustment during migration: src/cx/scope.rs (core region/spawn APIs take &mut RuntimeState), src/actor.rs (helpers take &mut RuntimeState), src/lab/runtime.rs + src/lab/oracle/* (lab owns RuntimeState), src/obligation/lyapunov.rs (snapshot extraction), src/observability/* (Arc<RuntimeState> diagnostics/inspectors).

Search recipes: rg -n 'ContendedMutex<\s*RuntimeState' src/runtime ; rg -n '\bstate\.lock\(' src/runtime ; rg -n '\bRuntimeState\b' src | rg -v '^src/runtime/'.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"DarkElk","created_at":"2026-02-04T21:03:17.356391261Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T02:18:59.217331156Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-31nlw","depends_on_id":"bd-1pfhx","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:13Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-31nlw","depends_on_id":"bd-1tc1m","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:13Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-31nlw","depends_on_id":"bd-2018r","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:13Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-31nlw","depends_on_id":"bd-2utc3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:13Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-31nlw","depends_on_id":"bd-3xvow","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:13Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-31nlw","depends_on_id":"bd-s13um","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:13Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":951,"issue_id":"bd-31nlw","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Extra inventory data point (ChartreuseCove):

Big-lock callsites summary:
- Total state.lock() occurrences under src/runtime/: 54
- Breakdown:
  - src/runtime/scheduler/three_lane.rs: 36
  - src/runtime/scheduler/worker.rs: 8
  - src/runtime/scheduler/local_queue.rs: 6
  - src/runtime/builder.rs: 4

Structural callsites of Arc<ContendedMutex<RuntimeState>>:
- src/runtime/builder.rs:942
- src/runtime/scheduler/worker.rs:35
- src/runtime/scheduler/local_queue.rs:29
- src/runtime/scheduler/three_lane.rs:295 and 782

Non-src/runtime/ RuntimeState touchpoints to keep in mind for migration / API reshaping:
- src/cx/scope.rs takes &mut RuntimeState for spawn/region APIs
- src/actor.rs helper fns take &mut RuntimeState
- src/lab/runtime.rs owns a RuntimeState (lab path)
- src/observability/* holds Arc<RuntimeState> (diagnostics)
- src/obligation/lyapunov.rs snapshot extraction from &RuntimeState

ShardedState / ShardGuard infra is concentrated in src/runtime/sharded_state.rs (mapping table + guard constructors).
","created_at":"2026-02-06T02:18:59Z"}]}

-{"id":"bd-32awe","title":"[FOUNDATION] Cross-crate type substrate integration verification","description":"Verify that all /dp/* crates correctly depend on and use the franken_kernel, franken_evidence, and franken_decision foundation crates, with no type forking or competing definitions. SCOPE: (1) Audit all /dp/* Cargo.toml files to verify franken_kernel is a dependency wherever TraceId, DecisionId, PolicyId, SchemaVersion, or Cx are used. (2) Audit all /dp/* crates to verify franken_evidence is imported wherever EvidenceLedger entries are emitted. (3) Audit all /dp/* crates to verify franken_decision is imported wherever runtime decisions are made. (4) Create a workspace-level integration test that imports all three foundation crates and verifies type identity (same TraceId type across all crates, not just structurally equivalent). (5) Document the dependency DAG: franken_kernel (leaf, no deps) -> franken_evidence (depends on kernel) -> franken_decision (depends on kernel + evidence). (6) Verify no circular dependencies between foundation crates. TRACING SPEC: INFO: crate {name} correctly imports {foundation_crate}. WARN: crate {name} defines competing type {type_name} (should use franken_kernel::{type_name}). ERROR: circular dependency detected between {crate_a} and {crate_b}. Metrics: type_fork_count (gauge, target: 0), foundation_adoption_ratio (gauge, target: 1.0). ACCEPTANCE: Zero type forks across all /dp/* crates. All three foundation crates form a DAG with no cycles. Integration test passes verifying type identity.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T17:59:40.386665699Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T00:19:58.235335778Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cross-crate","foundation","type-substrate"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-32awe","depends_on_id":"bd-1usdh.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:09Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-32awe","depends_on_id":"bd-3ai21","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:09Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-32awe","depends_on_id":"bd-qaaxt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:09Z","created_by":"import"}]}

All unit tests (10) and integration tests (2) pass.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["blocking","runtime"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-32ck","depends_on_id":"bd-1j64","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:49Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-32n6","title":"[ALIEN][E2E][HOMO] End-to-end: topology-guided exploration run + coverage report","description":"## Goal

End-to-end validation that persistent-homology scoring (topological novelty) improves schedule exploration *as a user experiences it*:
- explorer runs deterministically
- prioritization is explainable (evidence ledger)
- we get better bug-finding / coverage per explored node than baseline ordering

This harness complements unit tests for complexes/persistence; it validates the whole pipeline.

## Inputs

- A fixed corpus of deterministic lab scenarios (same as / compatible with bd-1ny4)
- ExplorerConfig knobs:
  - baseline: FIFO / existing ordering
  - topo: PH-guided ordering

## Required outputs (deterministic artifacts)

- A report per scenario and aggregate summary (JSON preferred) including:
  - explored nodes until first violation (if any)
  - equivalence-class count discovered
  - novelty score distribution over time
  - top-k "most novel" nodes with ledger snippets
  - runtime overhead attribution (time spent scoring vs executing)

## Logging constraints

- No stdout/stderr from core runtime code.
- Use trace events (Cx::trace) for internal scoring decisions; the harness may print a summary.

## Acceptance criteria

- Deterministic: identical outputs across repeated runs (same seed/config).
- Demonstrates a measurable improvement on at least one scenario (fewer nodes to failure OR higher coverage at same budget).
- Includes failure-mode reporting when scoring is disabled/falls back (e.g., analysis too expensive).","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:18:03.025357317Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T06:05:00.846357566Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T06:05:00.846292956Z","close_reason":"Added deterministic topology E2E coverage-report test in tests/topology_benchmark.rs","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien","e2e","lab","testing","topology","trace"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-32n6","depends_on_id":"bd-115y","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:35Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-32n6","depends_on_id":"bd-1ny4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:35Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-32n6","depends_on_id":"bd-3490","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:35Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-32n6","depends_on_id":"bd-3oqu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:35Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-32n6","depends_on_id":"bd-453l","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:35Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-32ot","title":"E2E: Multi-Protocol Integration Tests","description":"E2E tests exercising multiple protocol stacks simultaneously. Scenarios: HTTP + gRPC sharing runtime, WebSocket upgrade from HTTP/1.1, HTTP/2 + pool + TLS, concurrent TCP + UDP, timer-driven timeouts during I/O, graceful shutdown across all protocols. Log interleaved protocol activity with correlation IDs.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T18:13:55.498314269Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T19:36:16.039969169Z","closed_at":"2026-02-02T19:36:16.039870135Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","integration","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-32ot","depends_on_id":"bd-1dgr","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:24Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-32ot","depends_on_id":"bd-1phl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:24Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-32ot","depends_on_id":"bd-2i3y","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:24Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":1051,"issue_id":"bd-32ot","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Cross-cutting integration tests are the final verification that protocol stacks don't interfere. Blocked on transport unit tests (bd-1qxg) and E2E logging (bd-1mgn) because multi-protocol tests need both foundations.","created_at":"2026-02-02T18:15:12Z"},{"id":1052,"issue_id":"bd-32ot","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"LOGGING REQUIREMENTS: Every test function must: (1) call init_test_logging() at start; (2) use test_phase!("test_name") for the top-level test marker; (3) use test_section!("phase") for each distinct phase (setup, execution, verification, teardown); (4) use assert_with_log!() instead of bare assert!() for all assertions that compare values; (5) call test_complete!("test_name") on success; (6) log key intermediate state (e.g., connection count, region tree depth, bytes transferred) at INFO level with tracing::info!() so failures can be diagnosed from log output alone.","created_at":"2026-02-02T18:29:52Z"}]}

@@ -0,0 +1,8 @@
+# Seeds for failure cases proptest has generated in the past. It is
+# automatically read and these particular cases re-run before any
+# novel cases are generated.
+#
+# It is recommended to check this file in to source control so that
+# everyone who runs the test benefits from these saved cases.
+cc 1cee252383a86e7980756d0333c6f08df91ebc33393926b8ed100d663e39ec52 # shrinks to cal = 0.0, e_proc = 0.0, ci = 1.9197653306733418
+cc a4c72404135a608bb6064d3ab6d602f7c1b7c0c9a8020a4954c92c531261b555 # shrinks to matrix = LossMatrix { state_names: ["s0", "s1"], action_names: ["a0", "a1", "a2"], values: [0.0, 0.0, 9.330579907742003, 0.0, 0.0, 0.0] }
